title: Entra ID app registrations, scopes, and app roles
description: Covers OAuth scopes vs app roles, delegated vs application permissions, and why a valid token can still be denied without the right role/scope claim.
body: Use when securing an API with Entra ID (Azure AD). Do not assume a valid token implies authorization. Actions: distinguish delegated permissions (acting as a user, scp claim) from application permissions (daemon, roles claim); register the API's exposed scopes and app roles; the client must be granted and consent to them so the token carries the scp/roles claim; the API must validate audience, issuer, and the required claim. Validation signals include the token containing the expected scp/roles claim and the API returning 403 when it is absent. Risk boundary: granting admin-consented application permissions is a privilege change requiring review.
